name: Rapalogs Coverage Repair

scope.question: Which known source-completeness gaps for Rapalogs need repair, and does resolving them change the coverage verdict or public boundaries?

summary: Coverage repair staged source, study, finding, registry-watch, review, seminal-anchor, support-map, and coverage-assessment records for Rapalogs. The repair adds PEARL one-year rapamycin trial context, the Harrison 2009 mouse lifespan anchor, a 2025 vertebrate lifespan meta-analysis, a 2023 mTOR inhibitors aging review, active or completed no-results registries for REVERSE, Everolimus Aging, RESTOR, Rapa/cMRI, RAP PAC, and finding-level context for the already-public sirolimus older-adults registry. The staged outlook proposes a low-confidence human_functional_benefit read with mixed momentum, replacing the prior moderate activity-only human_biomarker_signal wording.

materiality_decision.rationale: The repair materially changes the public support map and boundary language: human functional endpoints are now visible enough to justify a staged human_functional_benefit label, but confidence should drop to low because PEARL is mixed, RTB101 has a phase 3 miss, several records are no-results registries, and chronic preventive-use safety remains unresolved.

search_log.query: NCT04488601, NCT05237687, NCT07354620, NCT05835999, NCT06658093, NCT04742777, NCT05949658, NCT06789900, and NCT07191353 direct registry checks plus rapamycin/sirolimus/rapalog aging queries
search_log.summary: Confirmed current status, enrollment, dates, endpoint scope, and no-results status. PEARL is completed with a peer-reviewed 2025 result source; NCT05237687 is recruiting with no posted results; REVERSE is active not recruiting with no posted results; Everolimus Aging is active not recruiting with no posted results; RESTOR and RAP PAC are recruiting with no posted results; Rapa/cMRI is completed with no posted results; RapaLoad and resistance-training/rapamycin bone trials were screened as boundary watch context.

search_log.query: PMID 40188830, PMID 19587680, PMID 40532901, PMID 37142830, plus existing Rapalogs support PMIDs
search_log.summary: Confirmed PEARL as the 2025 Aging randomized rapamycin trial, Harrison 2009 as the seminal Nature mouse lifespan anchor, PMID 40532901 as a 2025 vertebrate lifespan meta-analysis, and PMID 37142830 as the direct Nature Aging mTOR inhibitors aging review. Existing PMIDs 25540326, 29997249, 33977284, 29408453, 31761958, and 42169620 were used for local support-map repair.

search_log.source_name: Local public records and coverage assessment
search_log.query: Rapalogs outlook, May 30 surveillance bundle, June 11 coverage assessment, and linked immune-rejuvenation, senomorphic, and sirolimus records
search_log.summary: Compared the prior activity-only Rapalogs outlook against local human, animal, mechanistic, safety, limiting, and registry records. The repair prioritized direct Rapalogs support-map normalization, finding-level no-results watch records, review triangulation, and branch boundary language.

trial_watch_checks.summary: Staged the peer-reviewed PEARL result as the main new human safety and healthspan anchor; ClinicalTrials.gov itself has no registry results section, but the PubMed-indexed paper reports the trial.
Staged a finding-level no-results watch item because the current public outlook cites this active registry but lacks a finding to support source-map auditability.
Staged as active-not-recruiting no-results methylation-age and combination-intervention context for weekly rapamycin and fasting-mimicking diet.
Staged as active-not-recruiting no-results everolimus geroprotection context with metabolic, cardiac, cognitive, molecular, and safety endpoints.
Staged as recruiting no-results rapamycin and everolimus PK/PD context in healthy older adults.
Staged as completed no-results older-adult rapamycin cardiac-function context.
Staged as recruiting no-results rapalog dose, toxicity, PK, tissue target-engagement, and safety context.

excluded_sources.title: Everolimus and/or Exercise to Prevent Bone Loss in Postmenopausal Women
excluded_sources.reason: Direct everolimus and skeletal-aging context, but the trial is at the exercise/bone-loss boundary. It should remain on the watchlist after core Rapalogs repair rather than expand this bundle further.

excluded_sources.title: Resistance Training and Rapamycin to Enhance Bone Formation in Postmenopausal Women
excluded_sources.reason: Direct rapamycin and postmenopausal bone-formation context, but it is exercise/bone-specific and no-results. It should be reconsidered during a skeletal-aging or next Rapalogs registry pass.

notes: The staged repair intentionally changes evidence_stage upward to human_functional_benefit while lowering confidence to low; this is a boundary correction, not a strengthening of LEV relevance.
No-results registry entries are represented as regulatory/program-update findings with inconclusive direction.
Month-only ClinicalTrials.gov completion dates were normalized to the first day of the month in structured date fields and preserved as month-level timing in text notes.
NCT04488601 has a peer-reviewed PEARL paper even though the ClinicalTrials.gov API view has no registry results section.
The bundle does not publish any public records; it stages candidates for evidence and editorial review.
next_actions: Run source-fidelity, interpretation/forecast, taxonomy-mapping, and safety-limitations review for the staged Rapalogs coverage-repair bundle.

If approved and published, return Rapalogs to ordinary surveillance with special watch on PEARL replication, RTB101 interpretation, NCT05237687, NCT07354620, NCT05835999, NCT06658093, NCT04742777, NCT05949658, NCT06789900, and NCT07191353.
